label: 1636 CE
era_summary: 1636 (MDCXXXVI) was a leap year starting on Tuesday of the Gregorian calendar and a leap year starting on Friday of the Julian calendar, the 1636th year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 636th year of the 2nd millennium, the 36th year of the 17th century, and the 7th year of the 1630s decade. As of the start of 1636, the Gregorian calendar was 10 days ahead of the Julian calendar, which remained in localized use until 1923.

rome_italy.summary: Brecke records no major conflicts touching this region in 1636 (safety scored at the era baseline of 85). No Maddison GDP coverage for this region — economy held at neutral 50, reflecting the thin pre-modern record outside the European core. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).

andalusia_iberia.summary: Active conflicts in 1636: Spain-United Provinces, 1621-48; Sweden, Saxe-Weimar, Hesse-Cassel, Brunswick, Saxony, emperor, Spain, 1630-48; Comoros Islands-Portugal, 1633-37. Maddison records GDP/capita 1129 (PRT, 1636) — used as the economy proxy for the whole region. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).
